questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: research / this / significant / has / implications / study / for
options: This study has significant implications for research
Significant implications this study has for research
Research this study has significant implications for
For research this study has significant implications
questions.solution: This study has significant implications for research

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fatigue failure of a component typically occurs at a stress that is:
options: Equal to the yield stress every time
Below the static yield stress, after many cycles
Above the ultimate stress in one cycle
questions.solution: Repeated/fluctuating loads cause crack growth and failure below the static yield strength.

questions.subject: Mechanical Engineering
questions.stem: Diesel index relates to:
questions.solution: Diesel index = (API * Aniline point)/100. >50 = good ignition quality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about engine cycles is INCORRECT?
options: Increasing the compression ratio decreases the Otto-cycle efficiency
Otto efficiency rises with compression ratio
Diesel engines use higher compression ratios than petrol engines
Knock limits the compression ratio of SI engines
questions.solution: η_Otto = 1 − 1/r^(γ−1) increases with r — so the 'decreases' statement is wrong.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heat transfer by radiation becomes the dominant mode when the temperatures are:
questions.solution: Radiation ∝ T⁴, so it dominates at high temperatures (furnaces, the sun).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Orifice discharge coefficient C_d:
options: Ratio of actual to theoretical discharge. C_d = C_c × C_v
questions.solution: C_c = contraction, C_v = velocity. For sharp-edged: C_c ~ 0.62, C_v ~ 0.98, C_d ~ 0.61-0.63.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A short, stocky member under axial compression most likely fails by ____, while a long slender one fails by ____:
questions.solution: Stocky columns crush at the yield stress; slender columns buckle elastically first.

questions.solution: Ductile materials (e.g. mild steel) show significant elongation before failure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The second law of thermodynamics implies that heat, on its own, flows from:
options: A hotter body to a colder body
A colder body to a hotter body
questions.solution: Spontaneous heat flow is from high to low temperature; the reverse needs work (Clausius statement).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The purpose of a key in a shaft-hub joint is to:
questions.solution: Key transmits torque between shaft and hub.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow in a circular pipe is generally laminar when the Reynolds number is below about:
questions.solution: Below Re ≈ 2000 pipe flow is laminar; above ~4000 it is turbulent.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Free body diagram:
options: A diagram showing all external forces acting on a body
A diagram of all internal forces
questions.solution: FBD: isolate body, show all forces/moments. Essential for equilibrium analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Endurance limit of steel:
options: The stress below which it can withstand infinite cycles (≈ 0.5 × S_ut)
questions.solution: Endurance limit: S_e ≈ 0.5 S_ut for steel (up to ~10⁶ cycles). No endurance limit for Al, Mg.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limit gauge system checks:
questions.solution: Limit gauges: GO/NO-GO to check tolerance.
